title: Why Memex, and not a folder of markdown files
when_to_read: When the human asks why Memex / why this beats loose spec.md files, or when you want to articulate to yourself why following the Memex path is worth it.
body:
# Why Memex, and not a folder of markdown files

## The real bottleneck
In an AI-native team, writing code is no longer the bottleneck. The bottleneck is the round-trip from intent to outcome: saying what you want, settling the open questions, doing the work, and confirming you got what you actually intended. Most of that round-trip usually happens outside any tool, in chat threads, PR comments, and one-off agent conversations nobody keeps.

## Why loose markdown does not hold it
A spec written as a markdown file is a snapshot. It is correct the day it is written and silently wrong a week later, and nothing tells you which parts have drifted. You can satisfy every line of it and still ship the wrong thing, because prose has no way to check itself against reality. Here is the trap that catches even well-structured docs: a decision record, a design doc, a carefully captured rationale, with no empirical link to the code, is still just structured prose. It decays the same way a paragraph does; it just decays more legibly. For an AI agent the cost compounds: every session you re-read the files, re-derive the context, and quietly drift, because nothing durable is holding you to the plan.

## What Memex does instead
Memex turns the spec into a living contract, and gives it the empirical link that prose lacks.
- Decisions hold the WHY: each fork the work hinges on, with its options, trade-offs, and resolution. The rationale is a first-class object, not a sentence someone has to remember.
- Acceptance criteria hold the WHAT: the assertions the running system must satisfy, each bound to a real test that reports pass or fail back to the workspace. So the spec knows, continuously, whether the deployed code still does what the team decided it would. Verify becomes a query, not a vibe.

Acceptance criteria come in two kinds: scope criteria (plain-English outcomes a human commits to, set at the start) and implementation criteria (mechanism-level checks an agent spawns from each resolved decision, proving the code honoured that choice). A criterion that was green and turns red does not make the spec stale; it makes the spec informative.

## The five stages
Work moves through five gated stages, one at a time, and the gates are what keep it honest: draft (set out what "done" means), specify (settle the decisions and give each its implementation criterion), build (create and complete tasks until every criterion is backed by a passing test), verify (confirm it against the running system, harnesses green, before the PR), done (a human signs off). You do the work of the stage you are in; the spec advances only when that stage is genuinely complete.

## Why it is worth it, for the human and for you
- Documentation that cannot rot: the criteria are checked every test run, so the spec and the code cannot silently diverge.
- The rationale survives: decisions are first-class, so "why did we build it this way" stays answerable months later.
- "Done" means proven: at done the record is "we committed to outcomes X, Y, Z and here are the green tests proving we delivered them", not "we did some stuff".
- Speed without trading away quality: human and AI work from one source of truth, with durable context and a clear next move at every step, and the rails catch drift before it becomes rework.
- For you, the agent, specifically: you stop re-deriving context every session, you always know your next move, and your work lands provably right the first time instead of being second-guessed and redone.

## Answering the human
Answer plainly, from the substance above. Name the pain Memex removes (stale docs, lost rationale, passing review yet shipping the wrong thing, agent drift) and the mechanism that removes it (decisions as the first-class WHY, acceptance criteria as the test-bound WHAT, gated stages). You are not selling; you are explaining why the approach is sound. For the mechanics of any one piece, the per-topic guidance goes deeper.
